core_model.structure_summary: A nonlinear second-order wave equation with a sinusoidal restoring term posed on the edges of a metric graph, coupled at vertices by continuity and flux-balance conditions derived from conservation laws.
core_model.canonical_form: u_{tt} - a^2 u_{xx} + \beta \sin u = 0 \text{ on each edge}; \quad u_1=u_2=u_3 \text{ at vertex}, \quad \sum_k \tfrac{a_k^2}{\beta_k} u_{kx}\big|_{0} = 0

variables.meaning: a scalar field on each edge of the graph
arc-length coordinate along each edge of the metric graph
edge-dependent wave speed and nonlinearity strength, constrained by a vertex sum rule for integrability

deterministic_skeleton.summary: The single-edge integrable sine-Gordon equation: a second-order nonlinear wave equation with sinusoidal restoring term admitting soliton solutions.

notes: Core is the sine-Gordon PDE on a metric graph with conservation-law-derived vertex conditions; the sum rule a_1/sqrt(beta_1)=a_2/sqrt(beta_2)+a_3/sqrt(beta_3) makes the networked equation completely integrable and reflectionless. Skeleton strips the network to the canonical single-line sine-Gordon equation v_tt - v_xx + sin v = 0.
